rulings.Case Title: BURLINGAME INVESTMENT CORPORATION et al VS. HERMAN KWAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Disqualify Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT HERMAN KWAI'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel. Denied. Defendant never had an attorney-client relationship with plaintiff's counsel. Additionally, defendant's proposed rule that the attorney-client privilege remains with the owner of a corporation in bankruptcy when the court pierces that corporation's veil would frustrate bankruptcy law and prevent the trustee from maximizing the value of the estate. (Commodities Futures Trading Comm. v. Weintraub, 471 U.S. 343, 353). Moreover, the motion is untimely and it appears tht any legitimate concern that defendant might have should have been raised with the bankruptcy court in the context of objecting to the trustee's selection of counsel, and not this court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KARIN GREIER VS. PHILIP SODERQUIST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Response Against Pltf Karien Greier
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT PHILIP SODERQUIST Notice Of Motion To Compel Response Against Pltf Karien Greier Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and ANTHONY HARGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 and denied as to issues 5 and 7. Plaintiff has not opposed or, at least not meaningfully opposed, issues 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 and defendants have satisfied their burden of production as to those issues. Plaintiff produced evidence showing that there is a triable issue whether Defendant Harge acted negligently, thereby requiring the denial of the motions as to issues 5 and 7. Specifically, there are triable issues whether Harge was travelling at an excessive rate of speed, he failed to stop, he failed to ring the gong, and he was texting at the time of the accident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS MARCELLI VS. MOVIE MOVERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE MALPASO COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff concedes that its claim against the moving defendant, Malpaso Company Inc., lacks merit. The Court does not address whether "Malpaso Productions" is a legally recognizable entity, whether it is entitled to summary judgment or whether plaintiff's failure to name Mr. Eastwood is a fatal defect because none of those issues were raised in the notice of motion. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 8, PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES' Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration. Granted. The MOU contains an enforceable agreement to arbitrate that encompasses the underlying dispute. Although the MOU has not been submitted in a manner that is admissible evidence, Respondent does not dispute that the MOU is the governing agreement between the parties. Petitioner need not, at this juncture, present evidence supporting its underlying claim. rulings.Case Title: JANE GELFAND VS. POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jane Gelfand'S Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 9, Plaintiff Jane Gelfand'S Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for protective order is DENIED. Plaintiffs' articulated rationale for seeking a protective order - that defense witnesses could modify their testimony if they see the documents in question - is not an adequate basis for the relief sought. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Given the gravity of the privilege issues involved in this motion, plaintiff acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Dignity Health To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 17, Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Dignity Health To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber with modifications as follows: Hearings on the two motions are continued to Wednesday, October 25, 2012 at 9:30am in Department 302. The parties shall meet and confer in person to address each issue in dispute with respect to each discovery request and response. For each motion, the parties are ordered to prepare and submit by Monday, October 22, 2012 a joint statement or separate statements of issues remaining in dispute. As to each unresolved discovery request, the statement(s) shall set forth (1) the request of plaintiff (updated to reflect any modifications made by plaintiff through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (2) the response of the defendant to the request (updated to reflect any modifications made by the defendant through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (3) plaintiff's position as to the adequacy of the defendant's response; and (4) the defendant's position as to each request and its response. In addition, the parties shall submit proposed orders with respect to the discovery issues remaining in dispute, including as necessary protocols for addressing privilege, privacy, and electronically stored information.The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 17, Plaintiff Joseph Camillocci's Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Saint Francis Memorial Hospital To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber with modifications as follows: Hearings on the two motions are continued to Wednesday, October 25, 2012 at 9:30am in Department 302. The parties shall meet and confer in person to address each issue in dispute with respect to each discovery request and response. For each motion, the parties are ordered to prepare and submit by Monday, October 22, 2012 a joint statement or separate statements of issues remaining in dispute. As to each unresolved discovery request, the statement(s) shall set forth (1) the request of plaintiff (updated to reflect any modifications made by plaintiff through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (2) the response of the defendant to the request (updated to reflect any modifications made by the defendant through the meet-and-confer process or otherwise); (3) plaintiff's position as to the adequacy of the defendant's response; and (4) the defendant's position as to each request and its response. In addition, the parties shall submit proposed orders with respect to the discovery issues remaining in dispute, including as necessary protocols for addressing privilege, privacy, and electronically stored information. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. OCEAN BIO-CHEM INC DBA STAR BRITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT OCEAN BIO-CHEM INC DBA STAR BRITE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The Notice and Certificate of Merit complies with Health & Safety Code 25249.7(d.) The argument that he Complaint improperly includes products other than Star brite Liquid Electrical Tape (Black), while apparently conceded by the Plaintiff, is nonetheless not amenable to resolution on a demurrer since it seeks to adjudicate less than an entire cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. OCEAN BIO-CHEM INC DBA STAR BRITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 24, 2012, Line 13, PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Denied. There is no prejudice to Plaintiff, who failed to have a default entered prior to the filing of the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
